user_comment: Nice, thin, not see through, true to size Dress is surprisingly nice, I received many compliments on the dress. The material is thin but it isn’t see through. I could have pulled off a small, that’s if I wanted it to fit a little tighter but the medium was fine.

Cute dress seems more cosplay. Dress is Very light weight. Wife normally takes size 24 and she is 5’1. We got the 5X but we feel 4X would have been better. The cut / length was perfect. Perhaps it was the size but the chest area in the ad photos has a lovely pleated look but the dress itself didn’t really do that. I understand that each person’s body is different so perhaps it is just the difference between the model wearing it and my wife. She felt it was more a costume than a formal dress. Combined with a corset or sinch, sash, and boots could make a nice lady Pirate.

I would buy this again and get it in different colors! The white is not see through & material is very comfortable!

Cute dress with medium shaping Dress just arrived and I tried it on so note that this review won’t mention anything about wear over time, etc. Overall, the dress is really flattering and comfy. It does what I wanted and is true to size. I’m 5’10” and it reaches my ankle bone when standing which is nice. I think the biggest thing to keep in mind is that it’s medium control shapewear not super firm control. So you’re still going to be your shape, just held in a little. I still definitely have a mom pooch in this dress but it’s more smoothed out than if there was no shapewear. I also think it’s probably best to wear this with underwear that doesn’t give you any lines or cut into your sides at all so you don’t get a weird ripple shape because it’s so form fitting. I think this dress would be best for going out to dinner, family photos, and maybe even an evening wedding if you dress it up with heels and jewelry.

A Good fit made for all body types Dress met my expectations with a rating of 8/10 the length was perfect for the event I attended. The split on the dress was suitable and very tasteful. I ordered large I'm very top heavy I'm a 36HH and 235 lbs so I had to be taped (Breasts) to achieve the best results as a bra wouldn't have cut it. So being taped is for the absolute best outcome. I'm 5'3 so I don't have a super long torso so an adjustment needed to be made last minute with a dart In the right side where I decided to wear the sleeve as it can be worn on right or left as I decided. A medium would've been more suitable in my opinion because the fabric is forgiving really stretchy. Overall the dress met my expectations really comfortable and flattered my curves.

Cute with a quick delivery Dress runs true to size. The sleeves are a little long but I needed something quickly and this worked perfectly! Will need to steam iron it on low setting for a while to get the wrinkles out. I bought a large and am 5’5, 155lbs. I prefer things be a tiny bit loose after having 2 kids (mama belly pooch).

Dress seems to run true to size. I’m 6’0 and the length is about mid calf on me. There is some stretch to the fabric, it drapes nicely and is comfortable to wear. I may be to get this in another color! :)

Perfect for me! Dress shopping for my son’s Ring Ceremony at The Citadel and saw this online. Eyeing it for a while. Finally bought it and I’m so glad I did! I have a winner dress. I’ve bought 2 others before this and now I’m done. I’m 5’2” and length is perfect- I can actually wear with flats or very low shoes. No need to hem for me- I was surprised! It hugs the right places and I feel so comfortable in it. I bought a medium and I still have room to wiggle. This is a great find for me!

So. Many. Compliments. Dress size XS; height 5’ 1” weight 115lbs This dress was perfect my company holiday party. I got so many compliments on it from the color to the fit. It fit like a glove and hid my little mommy pooch from 3 pregnancies. The quality is great, it’s nice and thick without being hot and suffocating. I also machine washed and dried without issues before and after wearing it. I was nervous about getting the XS when the S was out of stock, but I’m glad I did get the XS because I think the S might have been a little loose in the tummy and not hid my problem area as well. My only minor complaint is that it rides up a little when walking and makes for an easy peep show when you sit if you aren’t careful with your knees, but that’s expected with short dresses anyways.

Good Fit- Cheap Material Dress Size: US 22 My Height: 5’10” My Weight: 235 lbs My Bust: 48” My Waist: 39” The fit was very flattering, and the ties at the waist make it fit even better. I am tall, and it still fell past my knees. It is also incredibly comfortable. But the material is pretty cheap. It’s basically just T-Shirt material, and I would not expect it to hold up well. At this price point, I would have expected something higher quality.

Good last minute wedding gown EDIT: this dress was BEAUTIFUL for my small outdoor wedding. I got so many (online) comments asking where I got it and saying it was beautiful. I felt comfortable and confident all day. I did have to hem it as I’m 5’ 3”. I did a super sloppy job, tucking fabric up under the belt, and the sewing on this gorgeous Etsy belt. It created a small train but was still easy to walk in. I just wish I would have remembered to steam the sleeves as they were a little wrinkly. Also wish it wasn’t 100 degrees that day. The lining did ride up a lot when I was trying on but had no issues day off. I did have to buy a slip though as it was a little see through. All photos look great though!! Bought this for a small wedding. Pros: flattering in the chest arms region, super easy to hem (tucked fabric under the ribbon belt and stitched), light and breathable, flowy, good stretch. Cons: wrinkles easy (nothing a steamer can’t fix though) and while it fits well in most places, it’s not the most flattering to my lower tummy... but that’s a my body thing.

Beautiful & Comfortable Edit. I purchased the Navy Blue. Fit perfectly. 225 5'9" 20/2xlThe dress is very beautiful and very comfortable. I am a little busty so I ordered a size up and had about four extra inches so I exchanging down two sizes.

Edit after wearing it: terrible quality EDITED REVIEW: Nope. Don’t even bother with this dress. After wearing it for an hour, the arms started snagging on the bodice. See picture to see the strings forming. I wish I got a picture after my daughter got home after a few hours. Her arms were basically stuck to her bodice. SO many strings. She quit trying to cut them. The dress is destroyed after only one wear. Such a waste of money. ORIGINAL REVIEW (before wearing): We’re having to pay $70 for a quick hem, but we’re in too much of a hurry to find a new dress. It’s beautiful, comfortable and modest, but soooo tall. It’s no longer much of a bargain considering the tailoring cost. But if you’re upwards of 6 feet tall with a short torso, this is the dress for you! Lol

Not very flattering for my shape Even though this outfit isn't the most flattering for my shape (hourglass- 45/36/49), it is well constructed and of good quality. JUST BUY IT! You won’t be sorry! First let me say that I am always very nervous about ordering any clothing online. I have bigger upper arms and a bigger butt! I have an hourglass shape and sometimes getting a good fit can be hard! I have the kind of shape that makes jeans “gap” in the waist when they fit everywhere else! So I was worried about this lace and my arms. I needed a cute, classy dress for a wedding we are attending in two weeks & I fell in love with this style immediately! After over an hour of reading reviews and trying to compare the height, weight & size bought by others who had purchased this dress, I was back & forth trying to decide between a medium and a large. I finally decided to go with a size large! (I am 37 years old, 5’6” and 170lbs. I normally wear a medium or larger or a size 8/10 (rarely a 12 if it runs small), like I said I have big upper arms, and a big butt). I got the dress 2 days after ordering it (Thank you Amazon Prime) and was first impressed by the quality of the dress! It is made of a thick material and doesn’t feel scratchy or cheap at all! I was so surprised by this given the price point! As others have said, it has a “rubber” line along the inside of the shoulders and the chest area to keep the dress from sliding down. It is a beautiful navy blue! The material is so soft, it almost feels like cotton, which makes it incredibly comfortable! The dress hits just below my knees (again... I’m 5’6” tall with average length legs). The large fits GREAT! I *probably* could have gotten away with the medium but the large doesn’t look baggy or frumpy at all. My husband said, “Wow Babe! That fits like a glove and you look sexy and classy!” So YES! I am very satisfied with my purchase. I will absolutely be ordering from this buyer again and I’m excited to try other dresses now! I know you’ve been reading tons of reviews at this point and are probably on the fence, so let me reassure you... JUST BUY IT! Other reviewers have said the return/exchange process was incredibly easy and FAST so worst case, you aren’t a fan, you got to try it on and you got a QUICK refund! I think you’re going to love it as much as I do though! Good Luck! Ps. I had no makeup on, hair was a mess and no shoes on so I cropped the pic so you can see how the large fits on my frame!

First of all this material is amazing quality! So much better than you’d expect from Amazon! I’m about 5’2, 185lbs, and 20 weeks pregnant. I bought my normal size large and it fit like a glove! Definitely true to size. It came up a few inches higher than my ankles being so short. I bought this color for my daughters promotion but I do think it’d look better in a lighter color. Overall GET IT!

Flattering dress, well made, received lots of compliments! First of all, I am 57 and worried this might be "too youthful" for me - but no! This dress is absolutely stunning! I am hourglass shaped, 5'7 155 and wear a size 10 or 12 in a skirt or pants. I thought about getting a large - but at the last moment selected a medium. It fit perfectly! Very forgiving in all the right places - flutter sleeves, tie at the waist for definition (and the waist isn't too high like other dresses on here!), layered ruffles, but not frumpy - the slit makes it feel updated and modern. Some info - yes, it wrinkles, but is very easy to iron using steam setting. I ironed it on the bed at the hotel in 10 mins, no problem. The waist is a little fitted, so you're likely putting this on over your head (just for gals who like to step into a dress). I had no problem with the snap coming open. It is slightly low cut, but you could always layer something underneath to fill in the "v." There is a liner, so if the dress blows open a little, no worries at all, you're covered to just above the knee. I was walking down a boardwalk to the wedding ceremony and had many compliments - I think this dress is really flattering and flows nicely as your walking! The dress is flowy, like the store photos show, BUT not light chiffon, as it may appear - it has some substance to it. I was at an outdoor wedding in a forest where it started at 75 degrees and cooled off in the evening - it was perfect for this event, I threw on a wrap. If it was 90 outside, this dress would be heavy and hot. As for the color - this is the gray blue. Sometimes it looks darker or lighter, depending on the light. I'm getting a few more in different colors! Hope this helps with your decision.

Great Quality First of all, i Loved this dress. It looked exactly as shown, the colour was a deep vibrant purple. The quality of the fabric is great, thick and sturdy with a lot of stretch. I am 5'3, 180 lbs, and i got a large. I could have gotten away with a medium for a more snug fit. The length was great for me just like i wanted it. I did wear a body shaper for a smooth finish. The only drawback with this dress is that it is unlined, and because of this, the sweat shows through in certain areas. I was mortified when i got to the church and there was a circle of sweat on my lower back area. I may try to get it lined for the next time i wear it. I do love this dress!

Color and fit made me smile! I love this dress!! First of all, I’d like to thank all of you for posting your pics of your dress for review!! It was so and because of that I was inspired to buy it. I will try to do the same more often. Ok, about the dress!! I actually love it! The 95% Poly 5% Spandex mix gives it a smooth draping over your body and would require easy care when traveling. So at 5’2” size 14/16, my frame is short. The plunging V line was a bit much for me so I put a tankini underneath and pinned the opening slightly together. With a 2 1/2 inch heel it needs no hemming. I turned the sleeves over once. The underside of the dress is solid white so if that bothers you to see it when walking, keep that in mind!! Otherwise, I’m looking forward to having somewhere to wear it when it gets warmer! 😊

Lovely Color & Design First of all, the color is FABULOUS! The fit was perfect. I am 5'6" and was very happy with the length of the dress. It was comfortable to wear and made me feel pretty! The fabric does snag easily. I got it snagged on a few things, but I think that is the nature of lace. I recommend this dress for those wanting something dressy, pretty, and comfortable.

Feminine fit, and flowy! First of all, the fabric of this beautiful dress is light, yet the bodice has a lining. The navy blue is such a nice, rich color, the A-line falls so sweetly to mid calf, and I love the sweetheart neckline and the cape sleeves. I wasn’t positive what size to order, because I usually order a size small (I am 5’6” and weigh 115 lbs.)—but with a dress that follows my shape, I didn’t want to be too perfect. I ordered a medium, and the fit gives me a tad more room, which is nice, actually, because I can always take it in a little. But I think I might have fit into a small, because my shoulders are narrow/medium. I didn’t want to take a chance, though. I recommend this dress; I think you’ll like it. 😊

Get it!!!! First of the quality of this dress does not feel cheap at all. I was actually surprised when I first held it. I was like oh wow. And then the dress fits perfectly besides the chest. I got a medium and I'm 140lbs and 5'3. Overall I love this dress!!!!

First off - it's a very pretty dress. I wore it to a friend's medieval-themed wedding and got lots of compliments on it. It fit rather loose on me. I'm 5'3" and the dress didn't reach the ground, but definitely came close. It's also a fun dress to dance in, the bottom part is very swooshy. The bottom has a lot of fabric so I had to arrange all of it whenever sitting down or getting up from a chair. A few things that you should know, that were the case with my dress, at least. 1. The smell. This is mentioned by the seller (smells like a thrift store). I washed the dress 4 times and it still has the smell somewhat. I ended up wearing a lot of perfume to mask it (which did work, but shouldn't be necessary). 2. There were lots of loose threads on the dress (see the photos). Given the cost of the dress, I wasn't expecting the most high level quality, but it's worth noting. 3. Bunched seams rip easily. Every time I got in and out of a taxi or sat down on a chair, some of the bunched seams in the lower skirt of the dress would rip. This was not any tight area of the dress, but the flowy bottom part. Didn't ever rip open the dress completely or affect the integrity of the dress, but it was an audible ripping sound and feeling each time it happened (5 or 6 times in one evening). 4. The bust was not sewn shut. See the photos with my review, but there was a small area of stitching holding the dress shut, but otherwise it's fully open. I wore a black undershirt to prevent any exposure, but just be prepared for this. Also the stitching closing the bust area was also coming slightly undone with loose threads. As I said, it's a gorgeous velvety dress for a good price, but it does have some flaws. It served me well for my purpose, just posting my honest comments about it so you can make your own choice whether or not to buy it. Also I loved the burgundy colour!

Give it a try! First off this dress has pockets! Thick material but very lightweight. It kinda a off white color. I received many compliments at a all white party. I ordered a 3x bit I could have definitely sized down. I'm 5'6 and the dress didn't dragged the floor.

Such a cute and well fitting dress First off, definitely size up if you are busty... Although the dress fit me really well, that is the most uncomfortable part of the dress. I ordered a medium based on the size chart and other reviews mentioning that the bust might be tight, so happy that I did. My measurements are 36/27.5/41.5 and I am 5'3" tall. The length is a bit longer than expected and the skirt a little wider as well, but I still like it. It feels really nice on, is not sheer at all, and seems well made, not to mention, the color is gorgeous.

The perfect dress!! First off, I am 5’7” and wear between an 8-10 in pants and wear a medium in shirts. I am also a size A cup. I ordered a Medium and it fit perfect! The only issue I had was I couldn’t zip it up myself. The zipper is on the left side in the seam. It is very comfortable and the material is amazing. Beautiful, well-made dress! A must buy!! First off, I have to say that the customer service from the seller is hands down the absolute best!!! They were very prompt in replies, understood my concerns and ensured my dress was perfect and delivered ON- TIME for my Prom that was scheduled last minute!!!! Incredible company!! Now about the dress.. I am 5’5”, 156lbs and wear a 36DD. I purchased a size 10. And it fits beautifully!! And let me tell you, this dress totally Complements me in all the right areas!! (Supports the breast beautifully!!) There is a front slit that starts from the hip down to the floor, but a little pin can totally fix this. It is perfect in the waist after you tie up the Corset. The dress is very well made, it feels great against the skin, and I am not worried about it causing irritation on the inside of my arms or coming apart when we dance. And the absolute part about this dress... IT HAS HIDDEN POCKETS!!!! I would highly recommend this dress and any purchases from this seller!! I will try to add better photos later, I was super excited to try the dress on that I just could not stand still!

Looks great but … First off, I’m about 182, 5’9”, and I wear a size 8 in dresses. I purchased a medium. The dress fits as expected, wasn’t too tight or too loose. Looks really great actually, but the material is a little stiff and it rides up A LOT when walking. It’s already above the knee and I have long legs, so it gets really short when I’m moving about, which becomes really annoying having to keep pulling it down. It’ll probably be best during the cooler months with tights. Other than that, it’s really pretty.

Almost a full 5 stars First out, the dress comes in a zippered bag, and I immediately noticed the quality of the material. I was thinking this was going to be thin satin, however it is more of a flannel backed satin - fine flannel but this makes this less showing of every pucker. The material made me say 'wow'. It is heavy and hangs beautifully like a very expensive dress, and the cut is also a classic but a saucy type of a classic so it gives the look of a designer dress. The length is above ankle on my 5'6'' body, perfect to flash a stylish shoe. My size is about a 14. I ordered XL. I am on the taller side, and pear shaped with a very straight figure. I hoped the dress might work and it did. The cloth hangs without showing dimples and such, but you do need some type of mild shaper under garment if you are middle aged like me :) to smooth out the bigger bumps. Given my slightly plump physique, it looks surprisingly good. Fits to size, but I will likely drop a little weight by the time the wedding comes around and this should look even better. There is only one drawback to the dress. The side zipper puffs out on the side while on the opposing side it does a little too but not quite as much. I believe if you are very busty this would fill in the gap, but I am average sized there so it looks off. I will likely fix this myself with a stitch or two but may hit the seamstress. Also I should say that the bust area does show a good peek but this is easily modified with an undergarment, or a pretty pin. I may do this, rather than flash too much. I really love this dress and I would say if you are taller with a straight figure it works even with a few extra pounds or a not so perfect figure.

I can see your decked halls First the bad: These pants are very poor quality. I bought the navy and they are so thin they’re almost transparent. I would expect that of a white pant but NAVY?! I pulled them out of the package and immediately saw my Christmas tree gleaming through the cheap fabric. Obviously anytime there’s light behind fabric it’s going to look a little crazy but this was bad without the Christmas tree exaggeration. The positives: short length is perfect for 5’3 in flats, which is a rare find. Apple shapes may want to size up based on the waist but generally this is probably true to size. The wide leg is very wide to me but again I’m a shorter person so this fit can be overwhelming regardless of quality.

Pretty, Not Flashy, Old Fashioned, Stately First, for reference, I'm 5'4 and 165 lbs. I normally wear a 12/14, Large or L/XL. Based on the size chart, I ordered the 14 and it fits great. Could actually slide the dress over my head without unzipping the side zipper. At 5'4 the hem almost brushes the floor without heels. I would have preferred a more midi length like the description photo but I'm sure the model is much taller than me. The material is nice and I'm sure will look 100% better once this garment is steamed. It's definitely giving me a matronly vibe. The sequins at least are somewhat muted and not ostentatiously cheap looking. I do think the price is a bit much for the overall look and quality.

Perfect wedding dress—just check it carefully upon receipt! First, I LOVE this dress—it was perfect for my cliff top/ beach elopement! Just be sure to inspect the dress thoroughly (top to bottom and inside), because I didn’t realize until months later…two days before our wedding…in a foreign country, that my dress had water stains at the bottom —all the way around the dress and dirt on the hem line. MY dress had been worn previously and repackaged as a new dress without being laundered first! Fortunately I’d already contacted a local laundry service to iron our outfits and they were able to fit me in—the day I arrived after 26 hours of travel. They washed and pressed my dress and it was so beautiful, so I feel lucky. BUT, the cost to urgently lean and press the dress plus a few Uber rides to drop off and retrieve my dress cost as much as the dress itself. Everything worked out and I focused on the solution, our special day, and the universe rather than this F up with Amazon’s repackaging team…or whoever was responsible for that. It’s not worth my energy to chase down any kind of reimbursement IMO, but I wanted to alert others to inspect carefully before hanging your (hopefully NEW dress) in your closet for your upcoming nuptials. The fit: I’m 5’ 4 1/2” and 127 pounds. I ordered a 4 and it fit like a glove. Snug across the back rib cage, so I went braless to keep my back smooth. With the breast padding, I didn’t need a bra anyway. Enjoy!!

Get the dress!! First, I will say I spent hours searching for a dress for a wedding. I wanted something that was dressy, cute, and made me feel comfortable and confident. I almost didn’t order this one because it’s tighter and I like on my stomach but I tried it. Get the dress. I am 5’2 and 160 lbs with a mom of 4 kids belly. It’s something I hate and always want covered. This dress was amazing. It’s tight in the right places for me (top and legs/butt) but was looser around the midsection. But the best part was was fabric over the dress that made it unique and also covered the part I didn’t want tight. It’s the perfect dress. I felt so confident and I also got so many compliments. I did wear some shapewear shorts underneath because I wanted a bit more smoothness but honestly I didn’t need it. And when I got hot later on, I took them off and it was just as perfect. If you’re in the fence, just get it.

VERY BRIGHT GOLD First, the good things about the dress. The quality was good. The fit was good. I normally wear a large, extra large, so I ordered a double X to make sure it would fit right and it did. It was very elegant. Now, for the not so good things. The material was a bit scratchy on my arms, chest and back area. It also made me look fat, but that could be my opinion or the mirror or the way I took the photo. Who knows! Maybe I am fat. Lol. The dress is very long I am 5’ 10 and it went to the ground on me. I think if you are not weird about how things feel on your skin like I am, or if you like very bright design, it is a perfect dress up gown. This is not a paid review

If you have large bust - Beware. First, this dress is STUNNING. Out of the package, every particle in my body was hoping this dress would be “the one.” My best friend is getting married in October and having a celestial theme but it’s been so difficult to find a purple MOH dress in the shades she loves. This dress could be perfect. From the flowy sleeve, the overall fit was perfect, it’s soft, comfortable, beautiful. BUT - it is BEYOND ME, why any vendor would cut a dress that would be used for more formal events this low and not have some kind of latch/hook situation as as option. In many reviewers pictures, it didn’t appear to be so low cut. I’m a 42G for reference, while I was hoping to have a reduction before the wedding (insurance sucks) I really thought this dress was going to make me feel beautiful but instead made me hyper aware of my body dysmorphia and there is no way I’d ever wear this standing next to my best friend at the altar.

Pretty, Sexy, but not really well made Firstly, I am 5'5" tall/122/35-29-37. I normally wear a size 4. I bought the S and it fits perfectly. It hugs in all of the right places. The red color is gorgeous. The design is elegant and fashion forward. However, the dress had several snags in the material. I was able to pull them through and they do not show, but I can tell that the material will be snag prone. It will be an on-going problem, and there comes a time with this kind of fabric that it does show and cannot be effectively fixed.. The dress was way too long for me. I would have to shorten it by at least 4". The top fits nicely but you have to "play" with it to get it to sit right. The strapped side actually sits slightly off shoulder. You have to pull the waist down to sit a bit low on the waist. It does not feel as though it would ride up once adjusted to the right spot. Once you get the hang of it, it should not be a problem. Overall, a nice dress that will suit formal occasions and be very comfortable to wear.

Absolutely GORGEOUS on!!!! Firstly: I NEVER write reviews. I bought this dress in the white and the black intending to keep one, but both of these beauties have a home in my closet from this day on! The fit is accurate to the chart, so follow it. Fabric is light, but not transparent or cheap-textured. Cotton/spandex with some stretch..enough that I could have ordered the L instead of the XL, but I like my clothes a little on the loose side. Stats: 64 years old, 5'51/2", 150-155lbs, depending; Bust: 38C bra (wearing a Carnival long line 36d in the pics as well as Cupid shapewear bottom...but I could have done without them.) Waist: 33", Hips: 42". I follow the charts for 40-33-42. I wear a size 12 dress and 10-12 pants (age-related butt atrophy, lol!!!) Bodice of this dress is LINED with a poly knit, so the pleats stay flat across the bosom, which is tacked with a stitch. Pic included. The waist actually measures in at a 35, plus there's some stretch, so it is generously cut. The skirt is not a full circle skirt (as in my Grace Karin dress,) but it's more of a 2/3 circle skirt, as pictured...that's the only point I may have changed. But that said, you can see that it accommodates a crinoline very prettily. I tend to be long-waisted, but this dress actually fell to my waist, unlike the Karin dresses which fall somewhere between my bust and waist. I'm not short - 5'51/2, and even with the crinoline the dress fell to just below the knees. All said: Great dress, great fit, quality manufacture, great price and a great value. Buy it, ladies, and you'll feel like a 1950's beauty!

Beautiful, comfortable dress with awful zipper For photo reference: I'm 5'4", 163 lbs., 40D bust, and in the photo I'm wearing 3" heels. The dress is size 14. After these photos were taken, I hemmed about 3 inches for it to be just at floor length without tripping me. (You can hem this dress up to about 4" without needing to sew anything; the overlay fabric doesn't fray, so it can just be trimmed evenly all the way around.) I'm mid-body change at the moment due to a medical condition, so none of my evening gowns currently fit, and I didn't want to buy a structured formal that won't fit me in two months. This dress is *perfect* for those in weight loss/gain transition, as it's well-designed to be flattering on a variety of body shapes and has enough stretch to handle weight changes. The empire waist helps conceal my slightly pudgy stomach, while the ruffled sleeves hide my squishy upper arms. Because of the stretch and drape, I think it will continue to conform nicely to my body even as I lose the medical weight I'm currently carrying. (Note that if you're a little on the busty side, as I am, it will give you a bit of a top-heavy silhouette.) Though there's nothing high-end about this dress (the fabric is not expensive, just basic sparkle stretch over a matte Spandex blend), it's great in other ways: It packs without wrinkling too badly, and it's incredibly comfortable to wear! It also falls just at the right point of the body that I can wear my normal bra with it and don't need special foundation garments. The only drawback is the zipper, which feels too delicate to handle the amount of fabric crammed into the seams on either side. It sticks frequently, and at one point actually stuck me in the dress because it had jammed closed; it took several minutes of careful manipulation to get it unstuck. I've had similar zippers break on me, and I'm dreading having to replace this one when it inevitably gives out (really not a fan of sewing zippers on stretch fabric, though I've done it a number of times). Great dress otherwise, but I docked a star for the zipper. Make sure you trim away any loose threads and keep the zipper lubricated with graphite or other fabric-safe lubricant so you don't put any undue stress on it.

I’d consider returning and sizing down but I’m not sure if the smaller size would come in the different fabric.

Ugh. Wanted to Love This… For Reference: I’m 4’11”, 120 pounds, with 34d bust. For reference: I am 5'8, 155 lbs, 38C bra and purchased this in size 12. I am wearing 2" shoes with the dress, and you can see how long it is. It is pretty long, so take that into consideration when purchasing that it might need to be hemmed. The inserts in the chest area were uncomfortable and just wonky, so the first thing I did was make a small hole to pull the inserts out and hand sewn it closed. Definitely not a big deal to spend 10 minutes doing that for such a cheap alternative to a more expensive wedding dress! I am also wearing a full length nude slip underneath because unfortunately, it is thin material and slightly see through from the waist down. The good news is minus everything above, it fit perfectly! It was so soft and comfortable. I live in Florida and was married in the summer, and it gets so hot and humid here. The dress was cool and flowy and exactly what I was looking for. I would definitely buy it again.

Pretty and comfortable. For reference: I am 5'8", 190ish lbs, 36DD, pear shape, 37" waist, 45" hips. I ordered the Black Grace Karin Sequin Dress in an XL and it is a comfortable fit. It is neither too loose nor too tight. There's some stretch to the waistline for plenty of breathing room. The dress is lined and not scratchy at all. The top of the dress sits well and is not restrictive. The zipper works great and I have had no issues with it sticking. The main thing to note is the length, it is a longer dress and may not work as hoped for shorter ladies. Overall, I am very pleased with this dress. I find it both flattering and comfortable. A win win, especially when you are on the larger side of the scale. Looking forward to wearing this at an upcoming event.

Not the picture at all For starters, I know that you take a chance when online shopping. However, for 50$, this dress is not it. However, this dress was entirely WAY too long (I’m 5’7 with long legs, so usually dresses look too short on me), and it was at my knees. The arms aren’t anything like the picture, and the chest size was HUGE. I don’t have a big bust, but some dresses that are XS I’m practically falling out of. I definitely recommend sizing down if you have a smaller chest, or sizing down in general. It was also see through on the back, and honestly kinda heavy around the bottom. The ribbons are nice if you want to flatter your waist, but again I would not reccomend buying it if you’re around my size. I’m 5’7 and 125lbs for reference!

Get them!!! Get them. I wear these all the time with everything! From the office to off duty. They give you an instant fashion boost when you put them on. Waist runs small Gorgeous bridesmaid dress! But I feel it runs a little small in the waist. I am large busted (40G) and 5'6" tall, normally wearing a size 14. I purposely ordered a size up to accomodate my bust, fully expecting the waist to be too big. However, the bodice fit me well and the waist was a bit snug. It won't be a problem when I wear shapewear underneath, but I'm glad I sized up. It's very long even with heels, and will probably need to be hemmed and ironed due to wrinkles from shipping. I definitely recommend going up a size if you are large busted or wide waisted. The lace is beautiful even with the mesh, which fortunately matches my skintone, so you cannot see the mesh on me. The lace is a bit scratchy and uncomfortable on the back of my arm, but tolerable for 1 night. Overall, I love the dress and feel it is a good value for the cost.

There’s a zipper on the side that you couldn’t really notice, and the frantic was really soft and breathable. I think the material, pattern, and cut made it flattering in the tummy area also. Happy with my purchase :)

Very Comfortable And Breathable! Got this dress for my maternity shoot at 33 1/2 weeks. I was 130lbs pre-pregnancy and was around 165lbs at the time of the shoot. I loved the way it fit over my very large belly lol. If I was any bigger, I would have needed to go up one size only because of the see-through lace part at the bottom being a little too high-up. Has great elasticity and isn’t see-through as long as you were the appropriate underwear. Wrinkles very easily, but I liked the aesthetic.

Great dress! Got this dress for my sister’s wedding. It worked beautifully. Covers all manner of sins Got this for my friend’s formal wedding. I am currently 5’3, 180 lbs, DD and the XL fit very well. The length was perfect which was nice, because I didn’t have time to get it hemmed! I ordered over 20 dresses and this was the only one I could tolerate myself in. The lace covers the arms…the black is slimming…the silhouette is flattering. The slit breaks up the black a little and shows some skin. My only gripe is that there are only spaghetti straps underneath the lace. So technically, your bra shows unlike what people said in reviews. I wore a black bra and black Spanx and luckily the lace makes it difficult to see unless you’re really looking. But ideally, the straps on the built in slip would be more tank top style. Anyway, overall this was perfect for the event and I got many compliments. I’m really not thrilled with my post-kids shape right now and this dress made me feel at least somewhat comfortable. One last thing, it does show w decent amount of cleavage so if that bothers you, might want to wear a slip or have fabric sewn into the neckline.
Beautiful dress Got this for my mom. She loves it. Great material. Great quality. She's 5ft 124lbs. Got a size small

Elegant-- may need minor adjustments Got this in black as an orchestra dress for a teen, and she loves it. Really has a beautiful shape, simple & classy, and it avoids both the "prom look" and the severe or frumpy look. It's also light and comfortable. Sizing was a little tricky. Normally would get a 6/8 but the 10 fit better around the waist and ribs, however now there is a little too much room in shoulder. You also may need to hem it shorter, depending on your preferences, because it touches the floor and has a train on a 5'7 person. (Photo shows dress with its original fit.) Still, it's really lovely and has a timeless look, so we think these minor tweaks are worth it.

My shoes were rhinestone platform sandals that made me about 3 1/2 inches taller and the dress was still a tiny bit long, so either prepare to wear very high heels or have the dress altered. Just FYI, I was sent the completely wrong dress the first time, so order early in case you need to do a return. The return was easy and Amazon got the correct dress to me just a couple of days later!

LOVE- measurements included in review for help height: 5’2 weight: 143# , athletic build waist: 27.5in hips: 39.5in (i don’t have “curvy” hips, i have hip dips, so mainly a bum measurement if that helps) bust: um, i LEGIT only wear sports bras-size medium, however, i do have a broad back this dress is BEAUTIFUL, true to size, only serious downfall (reason for the 4 stars) is that there is no hook in the back of the dress- it’s legit only a zipper, but i still don’t feel like that’s a good enough reason to not buy the dress because my zipper didn’t move/slide down at all.🤷🏽‍♀️ PLEASE get your size- it’s a bandage dress, so it stretches and hugs where it needs to, but not enough to go a whole size down for (i originally ordered a small because i usually have to due to having a smaller waist but it squeezed/flattened the heck out of my butt lol- medium fit perfect). if you have a larger chest, not sure how the “no support” will do for you.. it’s snug in the chest, but i would still have to pull my dress periodically throughout the day (however, i am more modest and feel like some things should be “left to the imagination” but yea)

Height: 5" Weight: 170 lbs Age: 71 Size: XL Color: Emerald Green My daughter is getting married in October and I wanted a dress that wasn't too blingy because I want to wear my mother's necklace which ties in nicely with the theme of the wedding. In June, based on the reviews, I ordered this dress in a medium and a large. Both were too tight (note: Koh Koh dresses tend to be made for Amazons--i.e. very long in the waist and very long in general) I was intrigued by this dress however, but being 5," I wasn't sure if it looked like I was wearing the dress or if it was "wearing" me. I took a photo and sent both back. I must've ordered 5+ more Koh Koh dresses but none measured up. I was also looking for Peacock Green but had no luck finding anything that fit. I also ordered from online bridal vendors but they were just "too much dress" and too much bling. Finally, in August I ordered this dress again in the proper size. It's not perfect--the armpits are too tight and it's too long--but as I'll need it hemmed anyway additional alterations won't be a big deal. I have one more dress on order from a major department store that cost 4x as much. It will probably cost that much to alter it. It's a safe bet that I won't be returning this dress and may even order one in another color!

No “online clothing ordering” risk involved Hello there! I am 5’8” and 182 lbs. I bought a size 16 and it fits PERFECTLY. I ordered this dress, and seeing that the delivery date was going to be in the middle of my vacation, I had it sent to my destination point. Risky move when ordering online, right? Well, it may have been bold, but man alive! When I tried on that dress I was so relieved that it fit so well, and was SO COMFORTABLE! I had packed another dress Just in case, but I didn’t even need to pull it out. This is my first time ever writing a review for anything I’ve ever ordered, but I’m just saying, this dress was well worth reviewing. My one suggestion is to ALWAYS measure before you order. Amazon has gotten really good at their size charts, USE THEM.

Absolutely Fantastic Pants Honestly, a 5/5 review for these pants. I bought them looking to supplement my flowy pants wardrobe :) I wasn't compensated in any way for this review. Lets start with how they feel: quality. How they look: professional, fantastic. Can be dressed up or down, a button up looks good tucked in, too. How they fit: exactly as ordered. I got the XS, my waist is 24", the band hugs perfectly. There's a clear button and a clasp on the inside to make sure the pants stay on. Length: adequate. I'm 5'7", and I often wear these pants with a pair of booties to get that shorter look that's "in" now. Just one thing - the rule of everything flowy - to make them look more professional, iron them, the 5 minutes doing so is absolutely worth it. I wore these to several events and many of my lady friends asked where I got them, they ordered some and were happy as well :) 5 stars. Stop shopping around, buy these. Totally worth it.

It’s almost perfect, except… I *nearly* had no complaints about this dress… until I looked in the mirror. The twist front is just a tad too small for my chest, so it looks like there’s a hole in the dress. No matter how much I tried to adjust the fabric, I couldn’t get it to sit right. And I don’t think I’m THAT large-chested (I’m a 38D). So, that’s something to be aware of before you buy this dress. If you’re around my size or larger, you may end up dealing with the same problem. Everything else about this dress I love, though. The sizing is spot-on otherwise; I ordered a medium and it fit great! The fabric is very soft and thick without making me sweaty, so it’s awesome for cold weather. That goes for the length, too— I paired the dress with some boots, so my entire body was covered and warm. Also, the color is very vibrant! I received quite a few compliments when I wore it to work. I really wanted to give this dress 5 starts, but the twist front ruined that for me. I don’t think it’s not worth trying for yourself, but just be aware that you may experience the same issue I had. If that’s a dealbreaker for you, you may want to reconsider getting this one.

LOVE this dress, but size chart is WAY off! I absolutely LOVE this dress - so pretty! I got it in eggplant for my daughter's wedding in October. Neckline is high enough to be modest without looking Victorian. It has a nice amount of stretch to it so that it doesn't feel tight or restrictive - even the sleeves are comfortable. It's several inches too long, but at 5' 3", I expect that. The biggest problem was the sizing! I went by the manufacturer's chart in the pictures, and I fell right between 18W and 20W. The advice I saw was to order down, so I got the 18W. It was enormous! I could pull several inches away from my waist. Just to be safe, I then ordered both the 16W and 14W. The 16W was still way too roomy. The 14W fits nicely - not tight at all, anywhere. It's got some breathing room, and eating room, too. I have not worn a 14 anything in decades - their sizing is weird.

Best. Dress. Ever. I adore this dress & I'm considering buying it in another color for a different event. I am 5'7", 145 lbs, and a 34B. Everything I read in the reviews said I should get a Small or Medium, & the size chart had me right on the fence between XS and S - I'm glad I ignored the reviews and went with my gut, getting the Extra Small (2-4) because it fits PERFECTLY. If you aren't quite sure what size to get, go with the size you'd normally choose with a top that's a little flowy, which for me is an XS. Stunning Dress, Consider Sizing Down if Between Sizes I adore this dress! As someone who's 5'6" and 190 lbs, I initially ordered an XL based on the sizing chart. The dress is made of a stretchy spandex material, which resulted in a slightly looser fit than I expected. Due to recent weight loss, I found myself between sizes, and in hindsight, I wish I had sized down for a more form-fitting look. Pros: Beautiful design and color Comfortable, stretchy material Made me feel stunningly beautiful. Versatile off-shoulder style Cons: Sizing may run a bit large Fit Tip: Consider sizing down if you're between sizes or want to accentuate your curves. However, if you prefer a more relaxed fit or want to conceal certain areas, stick with your usual size or even size up. I did wear shapewear underneath to achieve a smoother silhouette, which I'd recommend for the best look. Despite the sizing issue, I felt gorgeous in this dress and received many compliments. Overall, this is a fantastic dress for formal occasions. Just be mindful of the sizing and your personal preferences when ordering. Remember to check the size chart and consider your body shape when deciding.

Pear shapes rejoice! I agree with others to focus on your waist measurement - these are true wide leg pants. Wonderful Dress. I also have it in purple. I love the style and fit. I ordered an XL. I’m 5’4 and it drags on the floor.

The zipper was gone before I got to really try it on. I always check the zipper before putting the dress on a few times. This one was not going smoothly over the midriff seam (a common, but very annoying craftsmanship flaw). I was looking forward to this dress, wanted to wear it for the beach party. I still love the skirt, and hope to salvage it somehow. You need body-colored seamless underwear, the top could be supportive (I am 34DD, so it will have to stretch), but I did not get a chance to test it, I planned to use silicone pasties for the bra. The design idea and the material are great, just what I wanted, M fits me well (6-8), I picked it based on the chart. I liked the hourglass-friendly shape as well, the lightness, the fact that it is not lined. I know, most people order dresses like this for special occasions, and do not have time or often resources to fix zippers etc., so I do NOT recommend it.

What a fantastic fit I always wonder how stretchy the fabric is and sizing charts are always a bit tricky for a body type that can't fit the ranges perfectly. I decided on a small and was glad I did. My measurements are as follows (with a sports bra on) and this dress was perfect: Chest: 34 inches Waist: 28.25 inches (belly button level) Hips: 33.25 inches It has a bit of a stretch, a good long zipper, form fitting. A quick steam and I was ready to go. I love the neckline because it covers my chest and allows be to wear a variety of bras and it won't show. I love the fitted body and exposed arms. The color is a lovely rich burgundy and one of my favorite colors. I was in cold and hang dry and it dries pretty quickly. A quick steam after and it is ready to be worn.

Follow the actual measurements rather than the numerical sizes for accurate fit. I am 5'5, approx 145 lbs, and typically wear a medium or large depending on how clothing is made, but usually always in the 8/10 size range. With my measurements of a 38" chest and about a 32" waist, I decided to order the large despite it saying it was for US sizes 12-14, and it worked perfectly for me. I would say the Large fits more like an 8/10 and measurements in the listing are spot on. I didn't have high hopes for this dress, so I was pleasantly surprised. It is very comfortable and is like a thicker, soft style tshirt type of material. The material is stretchy, so if you order a size that is very fitting/too small, it will likely be unforgiving and require a smoothing undergarment to assist with lumps, bumps and overhang of skin that some of us develop after having babies and aging. This dress would work great for an event or night out. It is mature, elegant looking despite the comfort, and would be cute with flats or heels. Just make sure you measure yourself before ordering.

I ordered this coat in ***RED 2XL*** (Size 22) and it fits PERFECT! It is snug, but I am able to move freely! The red is a warm and BRIGHT Christmas festive! I get many compliments. "Machine Wash, Hand Wash Only" OPPS! Too late! I washed it in the washing machine, warm water and dried in dryer on low heat. Did not fade, did not shrink: I will not test this again, will take to cleaner. Material: It reads "wool" but not what tag says in coat - see photos. However, it feels like a thick felt, fairly soft and 100% acceptable to this very picky old woman. The lining is beautiful too. IMPORTANT: The sewing is done well! PRICE: Is it worth the price? With free shipping I supposed. Before all the inflation etc. this coat would cost about $50 As I am CHEAP yes I'm complaining a tad, but much too happy with the coat I will shut up about the price =)

Very flattering, comfortable dress I am 5'6", weigh ~150lbs, bra size from 36D-DD (nursing), typically medium/large shirt. There is some stretch/give to this so it allows for room to move while also hugging somewhat. Pros + Inside is lined very well in a softer material so it is comfortable to wear, I did not have any problems with the sparkles scratching me anywhere + Moveable- I wore this to a wedding I attended with my toddler; I was comfortable moving, grooving, and chasing a toddler all night + Very flattering on my figure, the black is forgiving while highlighting waist with the tie and bust with the lower neckline + Neckline accentuates the girls without being inappropriate + A good combination of light weight while having enough substance to hang down by itself Cons - there is a snap button in the V of the neckline to hold in place that kept snapping open- I sewed it in place (I am not crafty and have 0 experience seeing; I did this with red thread and a needle in less than 1 minute and it held) - sequence can snag on some materials I was worried about quality of this dress but overall am very pleased with the purchase; I feel like it was an appropriate value for the price. I genuinely can't wait to wear it again.

long split sleeve dress I am 5'7 1/2" ... usually wear a 0x/1x... 38DDD. This dress is quite lovely in person. I think it would be flattering on a wide range of sizes. I ordered the xxl. I like that the neckline is modest and the sleeve slits are nice to cover the wobbly upper arms of this grandma - and yet - stylish. The material is a poly/spandex blend... the tag says 95% poly and 5% spandex. The hem is nicely finished - I tried to show in a pic. The waist is a wide strip of elastic - quite comfortable and not tight at all. I don't wear heels any more, so I found the length perfect for me. Washing instructions say: "cool hand wash. cool iron on reverse side, do not wring or tumble dry. Do not dry clean. Lay flat to dry to keep shape." The only thing I could think that would make this dress perfect would be pockets!! (NO POCKETS in dress- making sure I don't confuse anyone...) I washed it on my machine hand wash cycle and laid it flat to dry. It doesn't wrinkle so it would be a great traveling dress. I am considering other colors or prints.

Review for Size 14 dress I am 5'7", 220lbs, 40DD chest with a post 2 c-section fupa (also lost weight super fast from 300+lbs in a year so my abdominal skin is loose). I purchased this dress with the hopes it would "slim" down my figure for upcoming Christmas photos. I am not disappointed at all. It definitely does what I was hoping for - but I expect any empire waist dress would do this. I do love that this dress ties in the back. So pretty! I was worried about how the chest would fit and the potential for showing cleavage. My chest does not fill out the top by any means. It's covered but if I "set" the top a certain way I could definitely see potential for cleavage. As I bought this for Christmas card photos, I am opting for the more conservative "setting" of the top of this dress to reduce cleavage. If you're looking to flaunt, I definitely think you'll be satisfied. The dress hides all insecurities and highlights the best assets. I love the material of the dress! Soft and flowy. Very comfortable. (Black heels are from Walmart. $10. Most comfortable heels EVER! They come with padding in them. You won't be disappointed).

The patterned dress in size large was a little baggy in the top but the solid pink in large fit very well also. So I could have gone with either size medium or large as both fit very well. My measurements would suggest that I would need a large in this dress but medium fit me very well. I love this dress and felt great wearing it at both events. The material is nice. There were some wrinkles when the dress arrived but I had it dry cleaned and they came out no problem. In addition, the events were 2 days apart and I did not have time to get it cleaned in between. Even after wearing it to both events it did not get wrinkled. I am very happy with this dress and I feel like the only thing that would have made it better is if it had pockets.

I got this for a formal/cocktail style wedding this October, and wanted something that would be pretty warm. I also wanted a nice winter dress for the office. This ticked the bill on both of those. I will say I feel it looks better in person, as black dresses don't photograph very well. And the material is not breathable (which I like for the purpose, but I understand it's not for everyone) so it will work in a drizzle and cooler weather. I'm very pleased with this dress. I also plan to use this dress as my formal dress for my Alaskan cruise next September! Oh! It is prone to wrinkles, but the pleats in the front are not super sharp, so I'd recommend washing it and letting it hang dry in the bathroom so the steam from the shower and the gravity can do its thing. Or, if you have it, hang dry and use a wrinkle spray. Who wants to iron anyway?

I really want sure about how I liked this dress, but on a limited deal I got it for under $15 in September 2024. My husband liked it but honesty he rarely says no to anything I put on. So I sent the pic to my 74 year old mom & she thought it was adorable. (She was a model for years and has a keen eye for clothes) My daughter in law was on the fence about this dress but she hates dresses so I went with mom’s opinion. It’s cheaply made, it’s a size larger than my norm and it fits as it should but when I took it off and put it on or moved my arms while wearing it I could hear little tears. I haven’t worn it yet as it is a thicker fabric and it hasn’t gotten cold enough yet but I will pair it with my Dansko Ugg type boots as shown in the picture. I like having something comfy and causal to wear on date night & to church without having to carefully hand wash afterwards. I would NEVER pay more than $20 for this dress, tbh it’s just not worth it. But for a limited time deal on prime with free shipping I think it’s a good value. I’d rate the quality below Walmart & Sam’s/Costco/BJ and for the price I can get something there that has a longer return window and be able to return if it rips or gets a hole. I would not be shocked if I only get one or two seasons out of this dress. But hey, you can’t expect amazing quality for under $15 Also, yes the tops of the sleeves have a weird poof. I will be wearing it with a denim jacket to cover that up or have my mother in law try and fix it with a needle & thread cause I’m not a fan of that either. My measurements: My bust is 38 inches and my hips are 32-35 depending on shapewear with a waist of 32-35. I recently discovered the nebility shapewear and now wear it everyday under all my clothes because it has a snap crotch and replaced bras and panties & chinches my waist by 1 inch. (Not wearing here).

Beautiful and comfortable I am not one to write reviews but had to with this dress. I was skeptical but the reviews convinced me to go for it and I’m so happy I did, I love this dress so much. It fits amazing and the design/ detail of the dress hides any of those imperfections (cellulite). A lot of the reviews that I read before purchasing, stated that the halter was too big and I do agree. I am 5’9, 170lbs and a 32 DDD. Thankfully my momma is amazing and shortened the halter straps for me. But other than the halter ( which is an easy fix) I have no complaints or negatives. The dress was also plenty long even with heels on which us tall girlies usually struggle with. The bottom of the dress from mid thigh down is sheer… but not see through. “ sheer when the light hits it just right”. I received so many compliments on the dress! Do not question this one. Get the dress!!! I rated a 4 only because I think with all of the reviews they need to adjust the halter measurements so we as the buyer do not need to have it done.Again… GET THE DRESS!

Lightweight cardigan I can wear in Florida I bought the brink pink cardigan to wear with this tank dress also from Amazon. Not sure I like it with the dress, but I have other items I can wear it with too. It’s soft, lightweight and not a short shrug or a long cardigan. It hits me just below my waistline. I like the 3/4 sleeves too. I’m a 42DD and my arms are on the larger side…I ordered the XL and it fits nicely. It’s surprisingly loose on my arms and the front lays beautifully. Pretty sequin dress I bought the burgundy sequin dress in XL. I usually wear a 14 dress as I'm about 5'4"+ and 160 lbs with a large chest (40D). The XL was fairly roomy and there is some stretch to the fabric. The dress is comfortable except for under the arms. Without the jacket on, the sequins scratch my sensitive skin on the arms. The length of the dress is just right, falling just at my knee. The jacket is very nice-- fairly sheer but adds a good look and a bit of warmth. The sequins on the cuffs of the jacket are a perfect touch. The jacket even has darts for shaping. I wish I could buy the jacket by itself! The zipper works very smoothly and I could zip it myself because there is enough stretch. The pattern of the sequins is quite pretty. If the lining fabric was a bit stiffer or thicker I think it would be more smoothing for lumpy bodies. My photos were taken without any fancy under garments. I would recommend something smoothing underneath if you're shaped like me.

Good Quality for the Price I bought the dress for a wedding. I was pleasantly surprised by how much I liked it when I came in. Fabric is thinner than advertised, but was not see through in camera flash. Dress was very comfortable to wear, very stretchy, not constricting at all, but this means there’s very little structure. It clings, not shapes, but the full bottom was very flattering on my mom tummy. The pockets do make the dress jut out slightly on the hips but are deep and functional if need be. Length was slightly longer than knee length but flattering. I am 5’7” tall. Sizing is very generous. I am 50.5”/47”/51”, which is larger than the biggest advertised size, but I ordered the 24 and it was great, but I could have definitely gone a size down even though my measurements were larger than the size I ordered. As a side note, this dress would work great for a breast feeding mom. The wrap style top is very functional, not stitched together. From some other review photos, I was afraid it would show too much cleavage, but it didn’t. Overall, I was pleased and would purchase again.

Elegant and so comfortable I bought the medium. I’m 5’3” and 115 but have a chest. It fit so perfectly and stretchy. Lace doesn’t itch. Had it hemmed up one hem. I ordered the dark green to go with my green eyes. I got so many compliments at the formal wedding I went to. Would highly recommend.

Size and fit and color(s) I bought the rust and terracotta rust dresses in Medium. I am 5'7" and weigh 155. The rust dress was a little tight through the hips and so it sat a little high, causing the zipper to bulge in back. Luckily the color that best matched my daughter's wedding colors was the terracotta rust, which is the darker of the two dresses pictured, and the one that fits me well through the hips. I like the weight of the satin, though it does get static cling. I'll be moisturizing my legs a lot the day of the wedding to avoid that. It could use an ironing, though less than you'd think from the way it was folded up for shipping. The terracotta rust dress is quite a dark color--less orange than some of these pictures look to me. I am very satisfied with the dress.

Great dress for a special occasion I bought this dress along with a few other options for a special occasion dinner. I ended up going with one of the other dresses, but I'm keeping this one! I did wear a push-up bra (38DDD) and snip the thread at the bust that holds the dress in a more modest shape because I was wanting to put the girls on display. I could see myself wearing this for a work event with a petticoat as long as I put the modesty stitch back in place and wore a regular bra. I probably wouldn't wear this dress to an outdoor event in the summer as the fabric is a little thick and doesn't breathe well, but it would be good for cooler evenings or in the fall/winter. Over all, really love how this dress looked on me and how it made me feel!

Beautiful but disappointing. I bought this dress for a friend's wedding that I'm in. Its a dinosaur rainbow wedding and orange was chosen as my color. Picking an orange dress that doesn't make you look like an inmate or a pumpkin being plus sized is most certainly a challenge. I was thrilled when I stumbled on this dress. I was a bit wary of the price as 50$ is a lot for a single article of clothing. Placed the order immediately and received order 6 days before eta. The color is beautiful, sort of coppery but in the light definitely orange. I am 5'8" and its slightly long but not bad and pools very beautiful on the floor. But thats where the good ends. This dress looks like someone's first sewing job. There are loose and attached hanging threads on multiple places of the dress. The ruffle that will sit on my shoulder in view has a wad of visible thread where the stitch was ended. And lastly where the left sleeve is attached to the dress is completely separated. For a total of 60$ with shipping I am disappointed in the craftsmanship of the dress, although pleased with the quality material. Thankfully I can fix these issues myself as I still need this dress for the wedding, but I will be more careful when ordering in the future.

Happy packing and having fun with friends!

Beautiful Formal Gown for an Affordable Price! I bought this dress for formal night on a cruise ship. So good I bought it 3 times I bought this dress in navy in 2018 for a gala, and again in black in 2019 for a funeral. Here I am in 2023 buying a third one in dark red/ maroon for a wedding. I guess it’s time I write a review. This dress falls into that rare category of being worth what you pay, but being able to pass for more. Unlike many dresses on here it’s thicker fabric, that lays correctly on the body - unlike the lightweight cheap options you usually find on this site, it hangs down and doesn’t cling. It has a nice lining layer underneath to act as a slip, and the pleats fall perfectly in front. I went with a size Large. I am 5’5”, and usually a size 14 jean and 34-36D bra. I added a belt I found here on amazon to break up my waist line as well. I always receive compliments on these dresses. It’s definitely a basic simple dress that can work in many occasions, but with the added value of being from amazon. Here’s hoping they don’t come out with more dark colors that warrant me buying a fourth version in the future.

Love it! Fit great! I bought this dress in royal blue for my daughter to wear to homecoming where a modest style dress was required. This dress was perfect for the occasion! It fit my daughter almost perfectly, which is saying a lot as she's a very tall and slim young teenager. She's 5'2 and the dress in small hit right below her knees. The Lacy material of this dress is silky soft and lightweight. The neck line is see through, the actual bodice of the dress hit a little low on her chest, but with the right bra, it works. Bodice is fitted at the waist, then flows freely to the knee with ample material. There is a matching underlining that prevents the dress from being see through throughout, the material is also soft and lightweight. The ribbon for the waist is not attached to the dress, nor are there sewn in loops to secure the ribbon to the dress, however, this offers more options on how to wear, with or without the ribbon, ribbon tied to the front, or as my daughter did, ribbon tied to the back. The ribbon did stay in place the entire night with no sliding or untying. Overall, I love this dress! The silky soft Lacy material is perfect for a dress or casual night out depending on the shoes worn! It fits as the sizing chart says, so measure before buying. I'm so glad I did! She received so many compliments for this dress, and was the only one wearing it, which in the small City we live in is difficult to do. The Royal blue is true to color, very rich, and very pretty on!

Gorgeous dress I bought this dress in the color grape (purple) in 2018 for my doctoral graduation ceremony and it is still my favorite dress. I always receive countless compliments on it and the quality is excellent so I know it will last a decade or more. I wash in cold water in a laundry bag and hang dry and it looks brand new even after 5 years. At the time of these photos my measurements were: 34 bust, 25 waist, 39 hips, I weighed 125 lbs and am 5'3", ordered size small.

Beautiful and heavy I bought this dress in the emerald green y-velvet floral option (the "color" name is so long) in a size XL. For sizing reference, I'm usually a medium or large on top and am currently either a 36E/36F. In bottoms and jeans, I'm usually a medium-large or a 8-12/29-31, and if it helps, I wear a size 6 or 8 in Lulu leggings. I'm 5'3" tall with a short torso, and my measurements are about 44"-33"-44" (bust-waist-hip) right now, give or take. I'd say this is TTS, or you could potentially size down. I got this to wear to a work holiday party coming up next month, and I felt this pretty velvet floral was the perfect choice for something like that. I sized up because I have a larger bust and wanted to make sure I had plenty of room, but I should have stuck with my usual size medium or large; either probably would have been good. My husband commented that this size looked really baggy, and I agree, though I'm sure I could still get away with wearing it. The material is heavy and absolutely stunning. I tried to take several pictures to really show the way the fabric lays on the body and the sheen the velvety floral pattern has, but I don't think I did it justice. The dress is double layered, as that floral material is actually sheer. The fluttery sleeves don't have a layer beneath so are just a tiny bit see-through, which I liked. The fit is pretty typical to a wrap dress, but I love that it has a slit. It isn't too obvious, so it's nothing you'll notice if you're just standing, as you can see in the two photos I took where I'm not really showcasing it - I honestly wish it was a little more obvious. As for the top, it has a little silver metal snap to keep it from falling open, but I really wish they'd instead done a black plastic snap, even though it wouldn't be as sturdy in the long run. I just feel the silver is super obvious against the dark fabric and I didn't personally love it. I would probably remove the snap all-together and just let the top stay a little more open, or maybe throw a little stitch in it to keep it where I want it. Not really a complaint since it's such a tiny detail and can be easily removed, but I wanted to mention it. By the way, this dress is a FAUX WRAP style, which it does point out, but I still wanted to mention it. My only real complaint about this dress is the price, but I guess for the quality, it isn't bad. It just felt steep for an Amazon dress. Overall, I really do love this dress but ended up returning it because it just felt too big. I have a few other dresses I'm waiting to try out, but if those don't work out, I'll probably reorder this in a smaller size. I'd definitely recommend it. I think this is really perfect for a holiday party and it's a more unique material than other dresses I've found on Amazon.

Beautiful and heavy I bought this dress in the emerald green y-velvet floral option (the "color" name is so long) in a size XL. For sizing reference, I'm usually a medium or large on top and am currently either a 36E/36F. In bottoms and jeans, I'm usually a medium-large or a 8-12/29-31, and if it helps, I wear a size 6 or 8 in Lulu leggings. I'm 5'3" tall with a short torso, and my measurements are about 44"-33"-44" (bust-waist-hip) right now, give or take. I'd say this is TTS, or you could potentially size down. I got this to wear to a work holiday party coming up next month, and I felt this pretty velvet floral was the perfect choice for something like that. I sized up because I have a larger bust and wanted to make sure I had plenty of room, but I should have stuck with my usual size medium or large; either probably would have been good. My husband commented that this size looked really baggy, and I agree, though I'm sure I could still get away with wearing it. The material is heavy and absolutely stunning. I tried to take several pictures to really show the way the fabric lays on the body and the sheen the velvety floral pattern has, but I don't think I did it justice. The dress is double layered, as that floral material is actually sheer. The fluttery sleeves don't have a layer beneath so are just a tiny bit see-through, which I liked. The fit is pretty typical to a wrap dress, but I love that it has a slit. It isn't too obvious, so it's nothing you'll notice if you're just standing, as you can see in the two photos I took where I'm not really showcasing it - I honestly wish it was a little more obvious. As for the top, it has a little silver metal snap to keep it from falling open, but I really wish they'd instead done a black plastic snap, even though it wouldn't be as sturdy in the long run. I just feel the silver is super obvious against the dark fabric and I didn't personally love it. I would probably remove the snap all-together and just let the top stay a little more open, or maybe throw a little stitch in it to keep it where I want it. Not really a complaint since it's such a tiny detail and can be easily removed, but I wanted to mention it. By the way, this dress is a FAUX WRAP style, which it does point out, but I still wanted to mention it. My only real complaint about this dress is the price, but I guess for the quality, it isn't bad. It just felt steep for an Amazon dress. Overall, I really do love this dress but ended up returning it because it just felt too big. I have a few other dresses I'm waiting to try out, but if those don't work out, I'll probably reorder this in a smaller size. I'd definitely recommend it. I think this is really perfect for a holiday party and it's a more unique material than other dresses I've found on Amazon.

Nice dress for price! I bought this dress last minute as a bridesmaid dress for my brothers wedding bc my other one wasn’t coming in on time and I’m happy I did! The fabric was not see through at all and good quality! I got lots of compliments which was nice considering how all women usually feel postpartum like I was. The corset and the gathering in the front were nice to help cover anything I was feeling insecure about! Glad I found this dress and ordered it last minute!

Compliments all night!! I bought this dress last minute for a family wedding and was so happy with my purchase so greatful it was delivered on time. Beautiful dress I bought this dress to wear to a destination wedding. I’m 5’7” about 135 and it fit like a glove. I would get it in another color for another occasion.

Perfect Wedding Attire I bought this dress to wear to a fall wedding. My husband had purchased a blue suit and I wanted a dress and shoes that would complement his suit. This dress is a beautiful navy blue, with a beautiful lace design. The hem is a hi-low hem which is very flattering. We got many compliments on how well we matched each other. The dress itself fit exactly as expected, true to size. I only gave 4 out of 5 stars for stretch because it didn't feel very stretchy to me. I did not buy it for the stretch feature and I did not need any additional stretch. If you are looking for that, I didn't feel as if this dress offered "stretch". The color is a true navy blue, which was exactly what I was looking for. I wore it for a few hours and it was very comfortable, no complaints. For reference, I am 5'7" and 130 lbs. I purchased a small, which is generally what I wear in tops and it fit perfectly! I would recommend this item to friends and I have already considered purchasing additional colors!

Super flattering! I bought this dress to wear to Universal Studios, along with a Harry Potter Gryffindor sweater. I am 165 pounds, 5'2" and a 36DD and the XL fit perfectly. I am also in my 60's and by no means skinny, and this dress is so flattering that I will be wearing it solo too, after things warm up a bit. Beautiful and high quality! I bought this dress with high hopes and it did not disappoint! The bodice is full lined and fit like a glove. The lace work was beautiful. I purchased a size 6, and for measurements, I am about 33” in the chest, 5’ 8”, 120lbs. And did not mend any alterations. (Since it is an empire waist dress, it is very forgiving below the rib cage.) The dress came packaged well and only needed a little steam to release the folding wrinkles in the chiffon. The only negative is that I ordered two sizes- one was never sent & I didn’t receive a notice that it wasn’t coming. (It was a smaller size and thankfully I didn’t need it!!) So, keep an eye on your account if shipping seems long. The dress came quickly, only a few weeks after ordering. Other than that- what a beautiful dress for a fraction of what it would have cost in a bridal shop or dress shop!

Soft, comfy and cute! I bought this dress with the intention to take my pregnancy bump pictures in weekly. I find myself wearing it all the time now. It hugs your body so nicely and it’s incredibly soft. I am 5’7 and weigh 125 lbs, I ordered a medium and it’s perfect 🤩

Beautiful but Doesn't Last I bought this during a frantic search for the perfect Fall dress for a last-minute family photoshoot. I had a very specific style and colorway in mind and this was the closest I could find style-wise that also came in my size. For size reference, I'm 5'6" and about 240lbs. I usually wear xl or 2x and, following others' reviews, I went with the 2xl. It was a little long (as expected) and I had to safety pin the chest area as it is a DEEP v neckline. Other than that, it fit great! It has just enough stretch to be forgiving in all the right places but it also flowed wonderfully. The only thing that made me give it only 4 stars is that after only wearing it twice, it looks like the fabric has worn in the the area around where my belt was. That's disappointing but, as I will likely always wear it with a belt, I'm hoping it won't be very noticeable.

When l pulled it out and tried it on l fell in love with it all over again. It very flattering AND forgiving. I hemmed the front, it was a bit too long, and tapered it to give a slight train. You can wear it on or off the shoulder as well. The sequins and design of the gown makes it classy enough that very little jewelry is needed (otherwise it starts to look gawdy.) As an event photographer rarely am I photographed, but people insisted l pose at least once. I'm so glad l did, because this gown was giving what it was supposed to give. Buy it, you will certainly love it!! 5'4, 168-170lb, XL (I like form fitting gowns HOWEVER, since l'm photographing in it I need to be really comfortable otherwise a L would be great. Even a M with a good body shaper would work.)

Homecoming Gala Dress I bought this for a fundraising Gala. I am 5’8”and 215 lbs I ordered an XXL. The elegant black dress with gold appliqué is a true showstopper. Wonderful dress, for any occasion I bought this for a holiday party, but am definitely going to wear this for many more occasions to come. I'm 5'3 and 275 lbs, with most of my weight in the belly area. The 3X fit wonderfully on me. It hits midway down my calf, and the fabric is stretchy enough that it could accommodate larger busts. I'm a 48C and the girls sat well in this dress with ample coverage. The off-the-shoulder sleeves cover enough of my arms that I don't feel so self-conscious about my floppy underarms. I dressed it up with a belt, but even without one, the cut of the dress gives the look of a defined waist. The fabric is surprisingly thick and actually kept me decently insulated on a 52 degree night. I've tried a petticoat underneath and decided it's not necessary (stylistically, it looks cute though). The dress has a great A-shape on its own. I love this and will probably get this in black too. It's a beautiful, classic, multi-use look. Could not recommend more. I felt and looked amazing in this dress, and received complements all night.

I bought this for my Prom this year, and let me just say…This is by far the best dress I’ve ever worn. For reference, I’m 5’5”, and a size 16 in dresses normally, but I sized up a few sizes because I was worried about it not being completely true to the measurements. It is very true to size, and I ended up having to tie the corset completely shut to fit in it lol. All I had to do was take in the shoulders slightly, and hem the bottom (I probably could have gotten away with the tea length). Other than that, this dress was absolutely perfect. To my surprise, this gown HAD POCKETS which I utilized the entire night happily, even though they were a bit too shallow for my phone to stay secure in. I noticed at the end of the night I had a small tear in the top layer of tulle just above my pocket , but that was most likely my fault, as my corsage bracelet caught onto it multiple times (because it was a metal bracelet). The cut of the dress is a bit too awkward for most bras, but the cups are boned, so I ended up just wearing pasties. The lining of this dress was very silky, and not scratchy at all, it was actually very comfy to wear, and the dress dried out very quickly after my group got caught in the rain (I know it’s not intended for this, but still, very convenient). Overall, I would give this dress a 6/5 if I could, I paired this with a pair of Star drop-style earrings, a gold necklace, black heels, and a gold headband, and received so many compliments on my outfit.
